hero_subtitle: Your building's private forum. Report issues. Document disputes. Hold landlords accountable. Build community.
what_desc: TENANTNET.NYC gives every apartment building its own private, timestamped forum — so tenants can document problems, coordinate with neighbors, and create a paper trail that actually matters.
feat_01_desc: Maintenance problems, safety hazards, lease violations — document everything with photos and timestamps that hold up.
feat_02_desc: Every issue gets a status: reported, acknowledged, fixed, or unresolved. Nothing falls through the cracks.
feat_03_desc: Every post is timestamped and attributed. If it goes to court, you have a documented record of complaints.
feat_04_desc: Direct links to your building's DOB violations, HPD complaints, ACRIS records, and more — pulled from NYC public databases.
feat_05_desc: Send documented complaints directly to your landlord, management company, HPD, or 311 — right from the platform.
feat_06_desc: Posts are attributed to unit numbers, not names. Your identity is protected. Organize without fear of retaliation.
how_step1_desc: Your building's tenant rep distributes a unique QR code to each unit. Scan it with your phone camera — that's your login. No apps, no downloads, no passwords to remember.
how_step2_desc: Report maintenance issues with photos. Flag landlord disputes. Read building bulletins. Comment on your neighbors' posts. Every action is timestamped and preserved.
how_step3_desc: Send formal complaints to your landlord or city agencies right from the platform. Access your building's HPD violations, DOB complaints, and public records. Build your case.
who_rent_desc: Document violations. Build your case for rent overcharges. Know your succession and renewal rights.
who_market_desc: Just because your rent isn't regulated doesn't mean your landlord can ignore repairs. Document everything.
who_nycha_desc: Track maintenance tickets that NYCHA loses. Coordinate with neighbors on shared building issues.
who_coop_desc: Board transparency. Maintenance coordination. Building-wide communication without the email chain chaos.
records_desc: Every building on TENANTNET.NYC gets automatic links to NYC government databases. Know what the city knows about your building.
roles_rep_desc: The building organizer. Manages units, moderates posts, invites management reps, and sends official reports to the landlord and city agencies. Every building gets one.
roles_tenant_desc: Post issues, upload photo evidence, comment on neighbors' posts, track the status of reported problems. Identified by unit number, never by name.
roles_mgmt_desc: Invited by the tenant rep. Read-only access to all posts, can comment but can't moderate or delete. Transparency without control.
cta_desc: Scan the QR code your tenant rep distributed. Or log in with the username and password you created during registration.
signup_desc: Whether you're a tenant organizer, a concerned neighbor, or a tenant association — submit your building and we'll set you up. It's free.
signup_contact: Questions? Press inquiries? Reach us at
